description: Physical schema for one root-search diagnostic row per completed engine search (MCTS, beam, minimax). Separate from observation.v1: observation.v1 is per-position training/analytics data, search-summary.v1 is search reproducibility and diagnostics.
notes: One row per completed root search; NOT a per-edge or per-simulation trace (those are out of scope for v1 because they can grow without a stable bound).
Parquet key/value metadata should include physical_schema, logical_schema, logical_contract, contracts_release, and contract_version.
physical_schema, logical_schema, and logical_contract must all be search-summary.v1.
contracts_release and contract_version should match contracts.json.release_version for release artifacts.
action_index = shape * 16 + position; policy_visits and root_q_values are dense over [0, 64); principal_variation entries are action indices in [0, 64).

policy_mass_kind names the meaning of policy_visits: 'visits' (MCTS root visit counts), 'multiplicity' (beam leaf multiplicity), or 'none' (minimax, whose per-move estimates live in root_q_values with policy_visits all zero).
root_q_values entries are nullable: null for a root move with no value estimate (e.g. an unvisited MCTS child).

root_value and every non-null root_q_values entry are in [-1, 1] from the root player's perspective; |v| == 1.0 is reserved for proven (terminal/mate) results, and every unproven estimate is clamped strictly inside (-1, 1).

Event counters are defined identically across engines: expanded_nodes (a state's successor set was computed), generated_nodes (a successor state was constructed), transposition_hits (a cached search result/subtree was reused via state-keyed lookup), canonical_dedup_hits (a generated state was merged with a duplicate without reusing any search result), terminal_hits (a state was determined terminal during tree search; rollout terminals excluded), tablebase_hits (always 0 until a probe artifact exists). A no-legal-moves node is both expanded and terminal. Identical semantics do not imply comparable magnitudes across engines; use elapsed_ms for workload comparison.
engine_checkpoint, search_depth, rollouts, beam_width, node_budget, time_budget_ms, seed, and elapsed_ms are nullable (engine-run configuration that may be absent).

Producers currently emit the draft label search-summary.v1-draft (JSONL debug form); the stable search-summary.v1 label is blessed by this registration and adopted by follow-up label-flip PRs in quantik-core-rust and quantik-core-py.
